title: Milium ciliatum
taxonomy: Milium ciliatum is classified as a species of grass within the genus Milium, which belongs to the family Poaceae. The genus Milium is further grouped under the subfamily Pooideae. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as grasses. Milium ciliatum is native to certain regions of the world and is known to thrive in a variety of habitats. Its classification as a species of grass is based on its characteristics, including its growth habit, leaf structure, and reproductive features.

intro: Milium ciliatum, also known as Himalayan millet or wild millet, is a perennial grass native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Milium ciliatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, making it a popular herbal remedy in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
